meta.description: Canonical reference library for Beacon website citations. Each entry has a stable ID used in HTML refs.

berger-2025-progress.title: Our Progress in 2024 and Plans for 2025
berger-2025-progress.author: Alexander Berger
key_quotes: All in, we committed roughly $50 million to technical AI safety research projects in 2024.
In retrospect, I think that rate of spending was too slow, and we should have been more aggressively expanding our support for technical AI safety work earlier; we're now playing catchup.

coefficient-2025-more-funders.title: AI Safety and Security Need More Funders
key_quotes: Philanthropic funding for climate risk mitigation, for instance, was ~$9-15 billion in 2023 (the most recent data available), which we estimate to be ~20x as much as philanthropic funding for AI safety and security in 2024.
Third, Open Philanthropy and Good Ventures are currently a concentrated share of AI safety philanthropic funding, and others can correct our blind spots and make new bets.

mats-cohorts.title: MATS Program cohort pages
mats-cohorts.notes: Cohort sizes: Winter 2024: 63, Summer 2024: ~90, Winter 2025: ~80, Summer 2026: 120. Total since 2021: 527+ researchers, 180+ publications.

mcaleese-2025-field-growth.title: AI Safety Field Growth Analysis 2025
mcaleese-2025-field-growth.author: Stephen McAleese
key_quotes: ~600 FTEs on technical AI safety, ~500 on non-technical AI safety (1,100 total), 70 organizations, ~24% annual growth rate.

kidd-2025-founders.title: AI Safety Undervalues Founders
kidd-2025-founders.author: Ryan Kidd
key_quotes: Great founders and field-builders have multiplier effects on recruiting, training, and deploying talent to work on AI safety.
The dominant incentive gradients in AI safety point away from founder roles.

bergal-2026-capacity.title: The Case for AI Safety Capacity-Building Work
bergal-2026-capacity.author: Asya Bergal
key_quotes: capacity-building as an area of work is still massively underrated relative to its impact.

fortune-2026-anthropic-pledge.title: Anthropic's Billionaire Cofounders Pledge 80% of Wealth

transformer-2026-anthropic-ea.title: Anthropic Employees, Philanthropy, and EA
key_quotes: Dario Amodei, Amanda Askell, and Jack Clark have all publicly signed the Giving What We Can Pledge.
Nearly 30 Anthropic employees registered for an EA conference in San Francisco...over twice the representation of OpenAI, Google DeepMind, xAI, and Meta Superintelligence Labs combined.

anschell-2026-flush-future.title: Preparing for a Flush Future: Work, Giving, and Conduct
anschell-2026-flush-future.author: Sam Anschell
key_quotes: It seems fairly likely that some causes EAs prioritize may receive significantly more money soon than ever before.
Founding new orgs or piloting projects that may be able to cost-effectively absorb lots of future funding may be especially valuable.
